title: Acilepis sutepensis
intro: Acilepis sutepensis, also known as Sutep Mountain acilepis, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Acilepis sutepensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are often used to alleviate symptoms associated with infections and inflammation.
taxonomy: Acilepis sutepensis is classified in the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. The genus Acilepis is part of this family and is native to Southeast Asia. Acilepis sutepensis is a species within this genus. It is a type of herb or shrub that is typically found in grasslands or open woodlands. The classification of Acilepis sutepensis is further refined to the species level based on its unique characteristics and features.
